title: Lightly Salted French Peanuts Canelé
description: A traditional French dessert from Provence, featuring peanuts and peanut oil, with a delicate touch of salt and a crispy exterior giving way to a pillowy interior. This vegan-friendly version uses a one-pot method to create the perfect canelé.

ingredients.name: non-dairy milk
ingredients.notes: such as almond or soy milk

instructions: In a medium saucepan, combine the peanut oil, peanuts, cornstarch, sugar, non-dairy milk, and salt. Heat the mixture over medium heat, whisking constantly, until it reaches a simmer.
Continue cooking the mixture for 5 minutes, or until it thickens and turns a light golden brown.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the vanilla extract, if using.
Pour the mixture into 6-8 canelé molds or ramekins, depending on their size.
Place the molds on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Bake the canelés in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until they are golden brown and crispy on the outside and still slightly soft on the inside.
Remove the canelés from the oven and let them cool in the molds for 5 minutes before transferring them to a wire rack to cool completely.
